 {"id":"bd-39ye","title":"NO_COLOR environment variable support","description":"## Overview
Respect the NO_COLOR environment variable across all ACFS scripts per https://no-color.org/ standard.

## Current Problem
- Scripts use colors unconditionally
- Users with accessibility needs can't disable colors
- Piped output includes ANSI codes
- Some terminals render colors poorly

## NO_COLOR Standard
- If NO_COLOR env var is set (any value), disable colors
- Also disable for non-TTY output (pipes, redirects)
- Simple, widely adopted convention

## Implementation Details
1. Create color helper functions in logging.sh
2. Check NO_COLOR and TTY status once at startup
3. All color output goes through these helpers

## Color Helper Functions
```bash
# scripts/lib/colors.sh
_init_colors() {
  if [[ -n "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] || [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' BLUE='' RESET=''
  else
    RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' BLUE='\033[0;34m' RESET='\033[0m'
  fi
}

color_print() {
  local color="$1" msg="$2"
  printf '%b%s%b\n' "${!color}" "$msg" "$RESET"
}
```

## Files to Audit
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (main color usage)
- scripts/lib/tui.sh (interactive elements)
- scripts/install.sh (progress output)
- All scripts that use printf with ANSI codes

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR=1 disables all colors
- [ ] Test piped output has no ANSI codes
- [ ] Test colors work normally when NO_COLOR unset
- [ ] Grep for raw ANSI codes to ensure none slip through

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/logging.sh (add color helpers)
- All files using hardcoded ANSI codes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:49.345301724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T04:01:25.739562837Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T04:01:25.739539473Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ye","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:01.194662910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3aa6","title":"Prevent gcloud 'bv' from ever shadowing beads_viewer","description":"Summary
- gcloud SDK installs a `bv` binary in `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin`.
- The SDK’s `path.zsh.inc` is sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`, so PATH can include gcloud’s `bv`.
- User requirement: **gcloud’s `bv` must never, under any circumstances, intercept/be invoked instead of beads_viewer `bv`.**

Impact
- High risk of running the wrong `bv` in interactive shells, non-interactive shells, CI, or cron jobs.
- Mis-executed `bv` can break bead workflows and cause confusion during incident response.

Evidence (current environment)
- `which -a bv` shows multiple `bv` binaries including gcloud’s:
  - `/home/ubuntu/google-cloud-sdk/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bv`
  - `/home/ubuntu/go/bin/bv`
- PATH is mutated by gcloud SDK via `path.zsh.inc` (sourced in `~/.zshrc.local`).

Root Cause
- gcloud SDK ships a `bv` command (BigQuery-related) that collides with beads_viewer’s `bv`.
- PATH ordering is not explicitly pinned to prefer user `bv` binaries.

Proposed Remediation (must enforce precedence)
1) Prepend user bins ahead of gcloud in shell init:
   - `~/bin`, `~/.local/bin`, `~/.bun/bin`, `~/go/bin` must come before the SDK.
2) Add an explicit `bv` shim at `~/bin/bv` that delegates to the preferred beads_viewer binary.
3) Add a hard alias in shell init to force `bv` -> `~/.local/bin/bv` (or preferred).
4) Add a health check command (or script) that fails if `command -v bv` resolves to gcloud.

Acceptance Criteria
- `command -v bv` resolves to user beads_viewer (not gcloud) in:
  - interactive shells
  - non-interactive shells (`zsh -lc`, cron)
- `which -a bv` shows gcloud’s `bv` only after user paths.
- Running `bv --version` matches beads_viewer’s version, not gcloud’s.

Notes
- The collision warning appears after `gcloud components update`.
- This is a safety/operations issue, not a GA4 issue.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:42:06.371685930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:22:24.883957326Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:22:24.883935966Z","close_reason":"Implemented bv() protection function in acfs.zshrc that bypasses gcloud bv, added ~/bin to PATH, and enhanced doctor.sh to detect gcloud shadowing. Shellcheck passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-3co7k.1.1","title":"Typography Scale Enhancement","description":"# Typography Scale Enhancement

## Problem Statement
Current typography scale tops out at 3xl (clamp to 3rem). For hero sections and 
large displays on widescreen, we need a 6xl tier that can scale up to 6rem while
maintaining proper letter-spacing and line-height.

## Background
Stripe and Linear use dramatic typography contrast in hero sections. Our current
scale doesn't allow for this level of visual impact on large screens.

**Current Scale (from globals.css):**
```css
--text-3xl: clamp(1.875rem, 1.5rem + 1.5vw, 3rem);
```

**Desired Addition:**
```css
--text-6xl: clamp(3.5rem, 3rem + 3vw, 6rem);
--tracking-6xl: -0.04em;
--leading-6xl: 1;
```

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Add CSS Variables (globals.css)
Add to the fluid typography section (~lines 120-128):
```css
/* Extra large display text for hero sections */
--text-5xl: clamp(2.5rem, 2rem + 2.5vw, 4.5rem);
--text-6xl: clamp(3.5rem, 3rem + 3vw, 6rem);
```

### Step 2: Add Letter-Spacing Variables
Large text needs tighter tracking for visual balance:
```css
--tracking-5xl: -0.03em;
--tracking-6xl: -0.04em;
```

### Step 3: Add Line-Height Variables
Display text needs tighter leading:
```css
--leading-5xl: 1.1;
--leading-6xl: 1;
```

### Step 4: Create Utility Classes
Add to Tailwind config or globals.css:
```css
.text-display-5xl {
  font-size: var(--text-5xl);
  letter-spacing: var(--tracking-5xl);
  line-height: var(--leading-5xl);
}

.text-display-6xl {
  font-size: var(--text-6xl);
  letter-spacing: var(--tracking-6xl);
  line-height: var(--leading-6xl);
}
```

### Step 5: Update design-tokens.ts
Export these values for use in JS if needed:
```typescript
export const typography = {
  display: {
    '5xl': 'clamp(2.5rem, 2rem + 2.5vw, 4.5rem)',
    '6xl': 'clamp(3.5rem, 3rem + 3vw, 6rem)',
  },
  tracking: {
    '5xl': '-0.03em',
    '6xl': '-0.04em',
  },
};
```

## Testing
- View hero sections at 1920px, 2560px, and 3840px widths
- Verify text scales smoothly without jumps
- Check that tracking looks balanced at all sizes

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/app/globals.css (lines ~120-140)
- apps/web/lib/design-tokens.ts (typography section)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5xl and 6xl font size variables defined
- [ ] Corresponding tracking variables defined
- [ ] Corresponding leading variables defined
- [ ] Utility classes created
- [ ] Design tokens updated
- [ ] No visual regressions on existing pages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-02-03T19:53:29.124313366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:53:29.124313366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["css","design-tokens","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3co7k.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3co7k.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:53:29.124313366Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3co7k.1.2","title":"Animation Variants Expansion","description":"# Animation Variants Expansion

## Problem Statement
The current motion module (apps/web/components/motion/index.tsx) has good spring 
presets but lacks:
1. Scroll-reveal specific variants
2. Stagger container variants for different use cases
3. Entrance animations for modals/sheets

## Background
Stripe uses subtle, purposeful animations that feel expensive. Our current set
covers basics but we need more nuanced options for specific UI patterns.

**Current Exports:**
- springs: smooth, snappy, gentle, quick
- easings: out, in, inOut
- fadeUp, fadeScale, slideLeft, slideRight
- staggerContainer, staggerFast, staggerSlow
- buttonMotion, cardMotion, listItemMotion

**Needed Additions:**
- Modal/sheet entrance variants
- Scroll reveal with blur effect
- Scale-up entrance for badges/pills
- Stagger variants with different delays

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Add Modal Entrance Variants
```typescript
/** Modal entrance - scale and fade from center */
export const modalEntrance: Variants = {
  hidden: {
    opacity: 0,
    scale: 0.95,
    y: 10,
  },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    scale: 1,
    y: 0,
    transition: springs.smooth,
  },
  exit: {
    opacity: 0,
    scale: 0.98,
    y: 5,
    transition: { duration: 0.15 },
  },
};

/** Bottom sheet entrance - slide from bottom */
export const sheetEntrance: Variants = {
  hidden: {
    y: "100%",
    opacity: 0.8,
  },
  visible: {
    y: 0,
    opacity: 1,
    transition: {
      type: "spring",
      stiffness: 300,
      damping: 30,
    },
  },
  exit: {
    y: "100%",
    opacity: 0.8,
    transition: { duration: 0.2 },
  },
};
```

### Step 2: Add Scroll Reveal Variants
```typescript

export const fadeUpBlur: Variants = {
  hidden: {
    opacity: 0,
    y: 30,
    filter: "blur(10px)",
  },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    y: 0,
    filter: "blur(0px)",
    transition: springs.smooth,
  },
};

/** Scale up for badges/pills */
export const scaleUp: Variants = {
  hidden: {
    opacity: 0,
    scale: 0.8,
  },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,

    transition: springs.snappy,
  },
```

### Step 3: Add Micro-Stagger Variants
```typescript
/** Micro stagger for pill/tag lists */
export const staggerMicro: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: {
      staggerChildren: 0.02,
      delayChildren: 0,
    },
  },
};

/** Cascade stagger for dashboard cards */
export const staggerCascade: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: {
      staggerChildren: 0.08,
      delayChildren: 0.15,
      staggerDirection: 1,
    },
  },
};
```

### Step 4: Add Presence Animation Helpers
```typescript
/** Get animation props that respect reduced motion */
export function getPresenceProps(
  variants: Variants,
  prefersReducedMotion: boolean
): MotionProps {
  if (prefersReducedMotion) {
    return {
      initial: false,
      animate: "visible",
    };
  }
  return {
    initial: "hidden",
    animate: "visible",
    exit: "exit",
    variants,
  };
}
```

- Check bundle size impact (should be minimal)

+{"id":"bd-3co7k.2.1","title":"BottomSheet Component","description":"# BottomSheet Component

## Problem Statement
Mobile modals that use traditional center-screen dialogs feel "web-like" rather 
than native. iOS and Android users expect bottom sheets for contextual actions
and detail views. Our current jargon.tsx uses a custom bottom sheet pattern that
should be extracted into a reusable component.

## Background
**Why Bottom Sheets Matter:**
- Thumb-reachable on large phones
- Natural gesture interaction (swipe down to dismiss)
- Maintains context (partial view of underlying content)
- Feels native on both iOS and Android

**Current Pattern (jargon.tsx lines 298-331):**
```tsx
<motion.div
  initial={{ y: "100%" }}
  animate={{ y: 0 }}
  exit={{ y: "100%" }}
  className="fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-50 max-h-[80vh] rounded-t-3xl"
>
```

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Create Component File
Location: apps/web/components/ui/bottom-sheet.tsx

### Step 2: Component Interface
```typescript
interface BottomSheetProps {
  /** Whether the sheet is open */
  open: boolean;
  /** Callback when sheet should close */
  onClose: () => void;
  /** Title for accessibility (aria-label) */
  title: string;
  /** Content to render inside the sheet */
  children: React.ReactNode;
  /** Maximum height (default: 80vh) */
  maxHeight?: string;
  /** Whether to show the drag handle */
  showHandle?: boolean;
  /** Whether to close on backdrop click (default: true) */
  closeOnBackdrop?: boolean;
  /** Whether to enable swipe-to-close (default: true) */
  swipeable?: boolean;
  /** Additional className for the sheet container */
  className?: string;
}
```

### Step 3: Core Implementation
```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useCallback } from "react";
import { createPortal } from "react-dom";
import { motion, AnimatePresence, useDragControls } from "framer-motion";
import { X } from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { sheetEntrance, springs } from "@/components/motion";
import { useReducedMotion } from "@/lib/hooks/useReducedMotion";

export function BottomSheet({
  open,
  onClose,
  title,
  children,
  maxHeight = "80vh",
  showHandle = true,
  closeOnBackdrop = true,
  swipeable = true,
  className,
}: BottomSheetProps) {
  const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
  const reducedMotion = prefersReducedMotion ?? false;
  const dragControls = useDragControls();

  // Escape key handling
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!open) return;
    const handleEscape = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === "Escape") onClose();
    };
    document.addEventListener("keydown", handleEscape);
    return () => document.removeEventListener("keydown", handleEscape);
  }, [open, onClose]);

  // Lock body scroll when open
  useEffect(() => {
    if (open) {
      document.body.style.overflow = "hidden";
      return () => {
        document.body.style.overflow = "";
      };
    }
  }, [open]);

  // Swipe to close handler
  const handleDragEnd = useCallback(
    (_: unknown, info: { velocity: { y: number }; offset: { y: number } }) => {
      if (info.velocity.y > 500 || info.offset.y > 200) {
        onClose();
      }
    },
    [onClose]
  );

  if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;

  return createPortal(
    <AnimatePresence>
      {open && (
        <>
          {/* Backdrop */}
          <motion.div
            initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
            animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
            exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
            transition={reducedMotion ? { duration: 0.1 } : { duration: 0.2 }}
            className="fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm"
            onClick={closeOnBackdrop ? onClose : undefined}
            aria-hidden="true"
          />

          {/* Sheet */}
          <motion.div
            role="dialog"
            aria-modal="true"
            aria-label={title}
            drag={swipeable && !reducedMotion ? "y" : false}
            dragControls={dragControls}
            dragConstraints={{ top: 0 }}
            dragElastic={{ top: 0, bottom: 0.5 }}
            onDragEnd={handleDragEnd}
            initial={reducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { y: "100%" }}
            animate={reducedMotion ? { opacity: 1 } : { y: 0 }}
            exit={reducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { y: "100%" }}
            transition={reducedMotion ? { duration: 0.1 } : springs.smooth}
            className={cn(
              "fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-50 flex flex-col",
              "rounded-t-3xl border-t border-border/50",
              "bg-card/98 shadow-2xl backdrop-blur-xl",
              className
            )}
            style={{ maxHeight }}
          >
            {/* Drag handle */}
            {showHandle && (
              <div
                className="flex shrink-0 justify-center pt-3 pb-1 cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing"
                onPointerDown={(e) => dragControls.start(e)}
              >
                <div className="h-1 w-10 rounded-full bg-muted-foreground/30" />
              </div>
            )}

            {/* Close button */}
            <button
              onClick={onClose}
              className={cn(
                "absolute right-4 top-4 z-10",
                "flex h-11 w-11 items-center justify-center",
                "rounded-full bg-muted text-muted-foreground",
                "transition-colors hover:bg-muted/80 hover:text-foreground",
                "focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring"
              )}
              aria-label="Close"
            >
              <X className="h-5 w-5" />
            </button>

            {/* Content - scrollable with safe area padding */}
            <div
              className="min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto overscroll-contain px-6 pt-2 pb-safe"
              style={{ WebkitOverflowScrolling: "touch" }}
            >
              {children}
            </div>
          </motion.div>
        </>
      )}
    </AnimatePresence>,
    document.body
  );
}
```

## Problem Statement
Form inputs in the app use standard labels positioned above inputs. For a premium
feel, we should support Material Design-style floating labels that animate from
placeholder position to label position when focused or filled.

## Background
**Why Animated Labels Matter:**
- Saves vertical space (label shares space with placeholder)
- Provides clear visual feedback on focus
- Feels modern and polished
- Common pattern users recognize from native apps

**Current State:**
- Basic input styling in globals.css
- No animated label component exists
- Checkbox component has aria-invalid support

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Create Component File
Location: apps/web/components/ui/form-field.tsx

### Step 2: Component Interface
```typescript
interface FormFieldProps {
  /** Input name for form submission */
  name: string;
  /** Label text (becomes floating label) */
  label: string;
  /** Input type (text, email, password, etc.) */
  type?: "text" | "email" | "password" | "url" | "tel" | "number";
  /** Current value (controlled) */
  value: string;
  /** Change handler */
  onChange: (value: string) => void;
  /** Blur handler */
  onBlur?: () => void;
  /** Error message (shows error state when truthy) */
  error?: string;
  /** Helper text (shown below input when no error) */
  helperText?: string;
  /** Whether field is required */
  required?: boolean;
  /** Whether field is disabled */
  disabled?: boolean;
  /** Placeholder (optional, label acts as placeholder when empty) */
  placeholder?: string;
  /** Character count limit (shows counter when set) */
  maxLength?: number;
  /** Additional className */
  className?: string;
  /** Input ref for focus management */
  inputRef?: React.Ref<HTMLInputElement>;
}
```

### Step 3: Core Implementation
```typescript
"use client";

import { useState, useId } from "react";
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "@/components/motion";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
import { useReducedMotion } from "@/lib/hooks/useReducedMotion";

export function FormField({
  name,
  label,
  type = "text",
  value,
  onChange,
  onBlur,
  error,
  helperText,
  required,
  disabled,
  placeholder,
  maxLength,
  className,
  inputRef,
}: FormFieldProps) {
  const id = useId();
  const [isFocused, setIsFocused] = useState(false);
  const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
  const reducedMotion = prefersReducedMotion ?? false;

  const hasValue = value.length > 0;
  const isFloating = isFocused || hasValue;
  const showError = !!error;

  const handleFocus = () => setIsFocused(true);
  const handleBlur = () => {
    setIsFocused(false);
    onBlur?.();
  };

  return (
    <div className={cn("relative", className)}>
      {/* Input container with floating label */}
      <div
        className={cn(
          "relative rounded-xl border-2 transition-colors duration-200",
          isFocused && !showError && "border-primary",
          showError && "border-destructive",
          !isFocused && !showError && "border-border/50 hover:border-border",
          disabled && "opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed"
        )}
      >
        {/* Floating label */}
        <motion.label
          htmlFor={id}
          className={cn(
            "absolute left-4 pointer-events-none origin-left",
            "transition-colors duration-200",
            isFloating
              ? "text-xs font-medium"
              : "text-base text-muted-foreground",
            isFocused && !showError && "text-primary",
            showError && "text-destructive",
            !isFocused && !showError && isFloating && "text-muted-foreground"
          )}
          animate={
            reducedMotion
              ? {}
              : {
                  y: isFloating ? -10 : 14,
                  scale: isFloating ? 0.85 : 1,
                }
          }
          transition={reducedMotion ? { duration: 0 } : { duration: 0.15 }}
          style={{ top: isFloating ? "8px" : "50%", transform: isFloating ? undefined : "translateY(-50%)" }}
        >
          {label}
          {required && <span className="text-destructive ml-0.5">*</span>}
        </motion.label>

        {/* Input */}
        <input
          ref={inputRef}
          id={id}
          name={name}
          type={type}
          value={value}
          onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)}
          onFocus={handleFocus}
          onBlur={handleBlur}
          disabled={disabled}
          required={required}
          maxLength={maxLength}
          placeholder={isFloating ? placeholder : undefined}
          aria-invalid={showError}
          aria-describedby={error ? `${id}-error` : helperText ? `${id}-helper` : undefined}
          className={cn(
            "w-full bg-transparent px-4 pt-6 pb-2 text-base",
            "outline-none placeholder:text-muted-foreground/50",
            "rounded-xl", // Match container border radius
            disabled && "cursor-not-allowed"
          )}
        />
      </div>

      {/* Helper/Error text and character counter */}
      <div className="flex items-start justify-between mt-1.5 px-1">
        <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
          {showError ? (
            <motion.p
              key="error"
              id={`${id}-error`}
              initial={reducedMotion ? {} : { opacity: 0, y: -5 }}
              animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
              exit={reducedMotion ? {} : { opacity: 0, y: -5 }}
              className="text-sm text-destructive"
              role="alert"
            >
              {error}
            </motion.p>
          ) : helperText ? (
            <motion.p
              key="helper"
              id={`${id}-helper`}
              initial={reducedMotion ? {} : { opacity: 0 }}
              animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
              className="text-sm text-muted-foreground"
            >
              {helperText}
            </motion.p>
          ) : (
            <span />
          )}
        </AnimatePresence>

        {maxLength && (
          <span
            className={cn(
              "text-sm tabular-nums",
              value.length >= maxLength ? "text-destructive" : "text-muted-foreground"
            )}
          >
            {value.length}/{maxLength}
          </span>
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

```typescript
  // ... existing props ...
  
  /** Whether the alert can be dismissed */
  dismissible?: boolean;
  /** Callback when dismissed */
  onDismiss?: () => void;
  /** Auto-dismiss after this many milliseconds (0 = no auto-dismiss) */
  autoDismissMs?: number;
  /** Whether to show countdown progress bar when auto-dismissing */
  showProgress?: boolean;
```

```tsx

  <button
    onClick={onDismiss}
```

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  if (!autoDismissMs || autoDismissMs <= 0) return;
  
  const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
    onDismiss?.();
  }, autoDismissMs);
  
  return () => clearTimeout(timeout);
}, [autoDismissMs, onDismiss]);
```

```tsx
  <motion.div
    className="absolute bottom-0 left-0 h-1 bg-current/30 rounded-b-xl"
```

## Problem Statement
The landing page (apps/web/app/page.tsx) has good static design but sections 
appear instantly rather than revealing as the user scrolls. Scroll-triggered
reveals create a dynamic, engaging experience that feels premium.

## Background

- useScrollReveal hook exists but is underutilized
- Some sections use basic fade-in on mount
- No staggered reveals within sections

**Reference:**
- Stripe.com: Sections fade and slide up as they enter viewport
- Linear.app: Features cascade in with staggered timing
- Vercel.com: Smooth parallax effects on scroll

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Identify Sections to Animate
Review page.tsx and identify major sections:
1. Hero section (~line 100-200)
2. Features grid (~line 300-400)
3. Tool showcase section
4. Testimonials/social proof
5. CTA section
6. Footer

### Step 2: Apply useScrollReveal to Each Section
```tsx
import { useScrollReveal, staggerDelay } from "@/lib/hooks/useScrollReveal";

function FeaturesSection() {
  const { ref, isInView } = useScrollReveal({ threshold: 0.1 });

  return (
    <section ref={ref}>
      <motion.div
        initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 40 }}
        animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, y: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: 40 }}
        transition={{ duration: 0.6 }}
      >
        <h2>Features</h2>
      </motion.div>
      
      <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-6">
        {features.map((feature, i) => (
          <motion.div
            key={feature.id}
            initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 30 }}
            animate={isInView ? { opacity: 1, y: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: 30 }}
            transition={{ delay: staggerDelay(i, 0.1), duration: 0.5 }}
          >
            <FeatureCard {...feature} />
          </motion.div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </section>
  );
}
```

### Step 3: Use staggerContainer for Grid Items
```tsx
<motion.div
  variants={staggerContainer}
  initial="hidden"
  animate={isInView ? "visible" : "hidden"}
  className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-6"
>
  {features.map((feature) => (
    <motion.div key={feature.id} variants={fadeUp}>
      <FeatureCard {...feature} />
    </motion.div>
  ))}
</motion.div>
```

### Step 4: Add Blur Effect for Premium Reveals
Using the new fadeUpBlur variant:
```tsx

  variants={fadeUpBlur}
  initial="hidden"
  animate={isInView ? "visible" : "hidden"}
>
```

### Step 5: Different Effects for Different Sections
- Hero: Immediate (no scroll trigger, already visible)
- Features: Fade up with stagger
- Tool showcase: Slide in from sides alternating
- Testimonials: Scale up with blur
- CTA: Fade up (simple)

### Step 6: Performance Optimization
- Use CSS will-change sparingly
- Ensure animations use transform/opacity only (GPU accelerated)
- Don't animate too many elements simultaneously
- Use triggerOnce: true to avoid re-triggering

**@use-gesture/react Usage (stepper.tsx ~line 169-193):**
```tsx

  ({ movement: [mx], velocity: [vx], direction: [dx], cancel }) => {
    // Handle swipe logic

);
```

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Identify Swipeable Areas

4. Any carousel components

### Step 2: Create useSwipeScroll Hook
```typescript
// apps/web/lib/hooks/useSwipeScroll.ts

import { useRef, useCallback } from "react";
import { useDrag } from "@use-gesture/react";
import { useReducedMotion } from "./useReducedMotion";

interface UseSwipeScrollOptions {
  /** Minimum swipe distance to trigger navigation */
  threshold?: number;
  /** Items per "page" for snap calculation */
  itemsPerPage?: number;
  /** Callback when page changes */
  onPageChange?: (page: number) => void;
}

export function useSwipeScroll(options: UseSwipeScrollOptions = {}) {
  const {
    threshold = 50,
    itemsPerPage = 1,
    onPageChange,
  } = options;

  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState(0);

  const scrollToPage = useCallback((page: number) => {
    if (!containerRef.current) return;
    
    const container = containerRef.current;
    const itemWidth = container.scrollWidth / totalItems;
    const targetScroll = page * itemWidth * itemsPerPage;
    
    container.scrollTo({
      left: targetScroll,
      behavior: prefersReducedMotion ? "auto" : "smooth",
    });
    
    setCurrentPage(page);
    onPageChange?.(page);
  }, [itemsPerPage, prefersReducedMotion, onPageChange]);

  const bind = useDrag(
    ({ movement: [mx], velocity: [vx], direction: [dx], last }) => {
      if (!last) return;
      
      const shouldNavigate = Math.abs(mx) > threshold || Math.abs(vx) > 0.5;
      if (!shouldNavigate) return;
      
      const nextPage = dx < 0 ? currentPage + 1 : currentPage - 1;
      scrollToPage(Math.max(0, nextPage));
    },
    { axis: "x", filterTaps: true, pointer: { touch: true } }
  );

  return {
    containerRef,
    bind,
    currentPage,
    scrollToPage,
  };
}
```

```tsx
}
```

## Problem Statement
Primary navigation and CTAs should be in the "thumb zone" - the bottom third of
the screen where users can easily reach with one hand. Currently, some navigation
elements are at the top of the screen, requiring uncomfortable reaches on large phones.

## Background
**Thumb Zone Studies:**
- 75% of users operate phones with one hand
- Bottom of screen is most comfortable to reach
- Top corners are "danger zones" (hard to reach)
- iOS tab bar and Android navigation bar are both at bottom

**Current State:**
- Wizard has bottom stepper (good!)
- Main navigation header is at top (standard but not optimal for mobile)
- Some inline CTAs are mid-page (OK for content flow)
- Fixed bottom actions exist in some places

**Goal:**
- Ensure primary actions are reachable
- Consider sticky bottom CTA on key conversion pages
- Audit all fixed elements for safe area handling

## Implementation Details

### Step 1: Audit Fixed Bottom Elements
Check all pages for fixed/sticky bottom elements:
```bash
grep -r "fixed.*bottom\|sticky.*bottom" apps/web/
```

Ensure all have:
- `pb-safe` or `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`
- Minimum 44px touch targets
- Proper z-index layering

### Step 2: Add Sticky Bottom CTA to Key Pages
For high-conversion pages (wizard completion, learn start), consider:
```tsx
{/* Sticky bottom CTA - mobile only */}
<div className="fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-40 md:hidden">
  <div className="border-t border-border bg-card/95 backdrop-blur-lg px-4 py-3 pb-safe">
    <Button className="w-full" size="lg">
      Get Started
    </Button>
  </div>
</div>

{/* Spacer to prevent content overlap */}
<div className="h-20 md:hidden" />
```

```tsx
    <NavTab href="/tools" icon={Wrench} label="Tools" />
    <NavTab href="/glossary" icon={Book} label="Glossary" />
    <NavTab href="/wizard/os-selection" icon={Rocket} label="Setup" />
```

Note: This is a significant UX change and may be out of scope.

### Step 4: Mobile Header Simplification
On mobile, the header could be simplified:
- Logo/title

This reduces visual clutter and keeps focus on content.

**Files to check:**
